title: Chapter 12 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of React Router!

questions.question: Which package provides routing for React web applications?
questions.explanation: `react-router-dom` is the package for web apps. `react-router` is the core, but you usually install `react-router-dom`.

questions.question: BrowserRouter uses the hash portion of the URL to sync with the UI.
questions.explanation: No, `BrowserRouter` uses the HTML5 history API (clean URLs). `HashRouter` uses the hash.

questions.question: What component should wrap all your route definitions?
questions.explanation: `Routes` (or `Switch` in older versions) wraps `Route` components.

questions.question: How do you create a link to the home page without a full page refresh?
questions.explanation: `<Link to="/">` is the correct component.

questions.question: NavLink adds an active class by default when the link matches the current URL.
questions.explanation: No, you need to provide a function or className; it doesn't add any class automatically.

questions.question: What hook is used to access URL parameters like `:id`?
questions.explanation: `useParams` returns an object with the parameters.

questions.question: In a route path, a dynamic segment is denoted by a _____ (e.g., `:userId`).
questions.explanation: A colon before the parameter name indicates a dynamic segment.

questions.question:
What will the following route match?

```jsx
<Route path="/products/:id" element={<Product />} />
```
questions.explanation: It matches any URL that starts with `/products/` followed by a segment, like `/products/123`.

questions.question: You can have multiple dynamic segments in one path, e.g., `/category/:catId/product/:prodId`.
questions.explanation: Yes, you can have multiple parameters.

questions.question: Which component is used to render child routes in a nested layout?
questions.explanation: `<Outlet />` is the placeholder for nested routes.

questions.question: A route with `index` attribute renders when the parent route matches _____.
questions.explanation: The index route is the default child when the parent path matches exactly.

questions.question: What happens if no route matches the current URL?
options: You can create a catch‑all route with path="*" to handle 404s
questions.explanation: A route with path="*" will match any unmatched path, often used for a 404 page.

questions.question: You can only have one level of nesting in routes.
questions.explanation: You can nest routes arbitrarily deep.

questions.question: Which hook would you use to programmatically navigate (e.g., after form submission)?
questions.explanation: `useNavigate` returns a function to navigate programmatically (e.g., `navigate('/home')`).

questions.question: To get the current location object (pathname, search, etc.), use the _____ hook.
questions.explanation: `useLocation` returns the current location object.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `end` prop in `NavLink`?
options: To ensure the active class is only applied when the URL matches exactly
To open the link in a new tab
questions.explanation: `end` prevents the link from being considered active for sub‑routes.

questions.question: You can pass state via a `Link` using the `state` prop.
questions.explanation: Yes, `Link` can pass state that you can retrieve with `useLocation().state`.

questions.question: Which component would you use for a navigation bar that needs to show which link is active?
questions.explanation: `NavLink` provides the `isActive` flag for styling active links.

questions.question: To handle redirects, you can use the `<Navigate>` component or the `_____` hook.
questions.explanation: `useNavigate` can be used to redirect: `navigate('/path', { replace: true })`.

questions.question: What does the `replace` prop do in `Link` or `navigate`?
options: It replaces the current entry in the history stack instead of adding a new one
It opens the link in a new tab
questions.explanation: `replace: true` prevents the current page from being saved in history (like a redirect).
